briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 6, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Urgent Warning: Planetary Safety Boundary Exceeded and Deepening Climate Threats
sections.content: According to several reports released today, current carbon emissions have already reached a critical level, exceeding the 'planetary safety boundary' by twofold [8, 13, 15]. This warns that a failure to address climate change-inducing greenhouse gases could lead to generational consequences [125]. Climate change is accelerating rainfall in Antarctica, causing cracks in ecosystems [4], and leading to widespread ecological disruptions globally, such as increased landslide risks due to permafrost thaw [126], disruption of flowering times for tropical plants [131], and tropical insects reaching their heat limits [129]. Furthermore, direct impacts of human-induced climate change are evident, including early spring heatwaves in the central United States increasing the risk of severe weather [130], and an extended allergy season in Washington State [123, 135]. In this context, criticisms regarding the five-year gap in carbon neutrality target timelines between Hyundai Motor Company and Hyundai Steel [37], delays in renewable energy projects in the U.S. [95], the Florida House's vote to repeal local government net-zero policies [121], and the UK's plans to cut climate finance [138] demonstrate a retreat and uncertainty in climate response policies, adding to concerns.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Domestic Carbon Neutrality Efforts and Climate Change Adaptation Strategies
sections.content: Domestically, various efforts to respond to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality are expanding. Yeosu City is conducting on-site inspections for hosting the 2026 UN Climate Change Conference Climate Week, laying the groundwork for international cooperation [1, 14], and the National DMZ Native Botanical Garden is strengthening its cooperation in climate change response [3]. Furthermore, the Wonju Climate Change Promotion Center continues to operate carbon neutrality weekend experiential education programs [2, 7, 11, 16], while the Wanju-gun Federation and Daejeon University are each holding climate change response education and seminars to raise public awareness [6, 9, 18]. In particular, the Chungbuk Office of Education plans to fully implement environmental education by operating all schools in the province as 'Carbon Neutral Schools' starting this year [28, 29, 30, 34, 43, 53, 55, 57, 71, 72, 78], and Gwangmyeong City is recruiting trainees for its '1.5℃ Climate Volunteer Corps' to foster future carbon neutrality leaders [22, 35, 56, 61, 77]. Collaboration between the government and industry is also active; the Korea Chemical Industry Association and the Korea Atomic Energy Research Institute are exploring carbon neutrality solutions for the petrochemical sector based on high-temperature gas reactors [23, 36, 48, 67], and Samcheok City is strengthening its foundation as a carbon-neutral hydrogen city through the dissemination of hydrogen electric vehicles [24, 25, 26, 69, 75]. Concurrently, various local governments, including Gwangju City [21, 59, 70, 73, 76, 79], Jeollabuk-do [40, 41, 50, 60, 62, 66, 68], Yangju City [20, 44], and Seogwipo City [52], are expanding the operation of the automobile carbon neutrality point system to encourage voluntary citizen participation, and various campaigns and projects are underway to promote carbon neutrality practices in daily life [27, 39, 42, 45, 47, 49, 51, 54, 58, 63, 64, 65].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition: Large-Scale Investment and Policy Support
sections.content: The transition to renewable energy is accelerating both domestically and internationally, with large-scale investments being particularly prominent. President Lee emphasized the need to rapidly and extensively pursue the transition to renewable energy [87], and the government is aiming to secure 100GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030, primarily focusing on solar and wind power [93, 94]. Bolstered by such policy support, Chungcheongnam-do Province has signed a 1 trillion won renewable energy investment agreement with Singapore's Vena Group, promoting the creation of an offshore wind farm in Taean [86, 89, 90, 91, 92, 96, 97, 100, 104, 106, 108, 113, 114, 117, 118]. Domestic companies are also actively participating in the market through restructuring their new and renewable energy businesses [84] and expanding AI-based ventures [82]. Market interest is high, with new and renewable energy-related stocks like HD Hyundai Energy Solutions and Kumyang Green Power showing strength due to factors such as oil price instability [99, 105, 111, 112, 115]. Furthermore, efforts to expand renewable energy deployment at the regional level are continuing, with Namwon City extending its solar and geothermal installation support projects to urban areas [102, 103], and Incheon re-challenging for designation as a 'Distributed Energy Special Zone' [81]. In the United States, the inclusivity of renewable energy deployment is also expanding, with the spread of 'community solar' projects involving tenants and low-income households [107].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Response Status and Key Challenges
sections.content: In the international community, diverse research and responses to climate change and its impacts are underway. On a positive note, an analysis indicates that the UK's greenhouse gas emissions decreased by 2.4% in 2025, reaching their lowest level in 400 years, with coal consumption also falling to an all-time low [139]. This serves as a tangible example of successful carbon reduction efforts. Meanwhile, new research suggesting that climate change has increased snowfall in northern Japan by 7% [133] indicates that climate change can manifest in vastly different ways regionally. In the United States and Canada, the 'Climate Change and Human Health Seed Grant Program' is operating, supporting research and responses concerning the impact of climate change on human health [136]. At the corporate level, Duke Energy is investing in new battery storage facilities to expand the utilization of renewable energy [137]. Furthermore, news that Louisiana is joining the 'fight' against climate change-related lawsuits [134] demonstrates that discussions on the legal and social responsibilities of climate change are actively progressing internationally.

briefing.closing: As confirmed in today's briefing, the climate crisis is having severe impacts across all sectors of our society. However, simultaneously, diverse domestic and international efforts and investments are actively underway to overcome it. Only continuous monitoring and proactive action will be able to create a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.
